evidences.content: (Jerusalem) – Israel began major ground operations in the Gaza Strip on October 28, 2023. These violations were never prosecuted. Palestinian armed groups also deliberately launched numerous rocket attacks towards Israeli communities in 2014, which are war crimes. They also deployed and kept munitions in areas that unnecessarily put civilians at risk. This equates to approximately five attacks per day with the vast majority of incidents perpetrated by Israeli Defence Forces (IDF). evidences.content: ## How Hamas built a force to attack Israel on 7 October The report says the military regarded Gaza as a secondary security threat, with priority given to Iran and Hezbollah. Its policy towards Gaza, it says, was "paradoxical: Hamas was illegitimate, yet there was no effort to develop an alternative". The military had chosen a "conflict management" approach to dealing with Gaza, it says. And had assumed that Hamas was "neither interested [in] nor preparing for a large-scale war" - a perception reinforced by Hamas's own deception tactics. "There was no deep discussion of the question: What if we are wrong?" the report says. Over time, "a significant and continuous gap between the intelligence assessments of Hamas and reality" had developed. Previous attacks with far fewer casualties were used to justify military operations, including ground invasions. Three days after the Coastal Road Massacre, the IDF launched Operation Litani, invading Lebanon and attempting to push the PLO back beyond the Litani River. This operation resulted in several hundred Lebanese fatalities and displaced more than 200,000 Lebanese civilians. evidences.title: ADL Debunk: Myths and False Narratives About the Israel-Hamas War
evidences.content: UPDATED November 3, 2023: Above: Conspiratorial commentator Jackson Hinkle shares false claim on Telegram FALSE NARRATIVE: Yemen has officially declared war on Israel On October 31, 2023, Houthi rebels in Yemen announced they were responsible for missile attacks on Israel in support of Palestine. Soon after, rumors on social media alleged that the attacks signified Yemen’s official declaration of war on Israel. REALITY: Yemen has not declared war on Israel. The Houthi rebels responsible for the attack do not represent the country’s formally recognized government. Claims about Yemen’s supposed declaration of war appeared to come from non-credible accounts on social media rather than verified news outlets.
